summary: Solid architecture — single onShaderError registration, dedicated collector, renderer.compile() for eager validation. Four targeted issues need addressing before implementation: unhandled throw inside rAF kills the loop, interface vs duck-typing inconsistency, missing error-recovery UX, and a minor gap in optional cleanup handling.
strengths: Single onShaderError registration point in scene.ts avoids callback conflicts
ShaderErrorCollector as a standalone class is clean, testable, and decoupled from the renderer
renderer.compile() for synchronous ahead-of-render validation is the correct Three.js API (available since r152, project uses 0.183.2)
initAllForValidation/cleanupInactive pattern correctly validates ALL shader programs without permanently inflating the scene graph
Test strategy honestly separates unit-testable logic (collector, validation plumbing) from GPU-dependent integration that requires a real browser
Risks section correctly identifies the re-init contract and acknowledges that mode transitions already handle lazy init at modeManager.ts:103

issues.title: Unhandled throw inside requestAnimationFrame kills the render loop
issues.detail: validateShaderCompilation throws on error (Step 2). This throw propagates out of the rAF callback at renderLoop.ts:98, which means no further requestAnimationFrame(frame) call is made — the entire render loop dies silently. The user sees a frozen black screen with no indication of what happened.
issues.recommendation: Wrap the validation call in a try/catch inside the frame callback. On catch: log the error, optionally surface a visible 'shader error' indicator (e.g., a DOM element similar to the WebGL-unavailable fallback in main.ts:34-40), and either continue the loop with whatever systems DID compile, or gracefully stop. Do NOT let an uncaught throw escape the rAF callback.

issues.title: Plan simultaneously adds methods to ModeManager interface AND duck-types in renderLoop
issues.detail: Step 3 says to add initAllForValidation and cleanupInactive to the ModeManager interface AND to duck-type check for initAllForValidation in renderLoop.ts. These are contradictory: if it's on the interface, duck-typing is unnecessary; if duck-typed, it shouldn't be on the interface. Since LoopDeps.geometrySystem is typed as GeometrySystem (not ModeManager), the duck-type approach is the correct one — but then the ModeManager interface section should say 'add to the implementation only' not 'update the ModeManager interface'.
issues.recommendation: Keep initAllForValidation and cleanupInactive as implementation-only methods on the object returned by createModeManager. In renderLoop.ts, use a type guard like `'initAllForValidation' in d.geometrySystem` to detect ModeManager. Do not add these methods to the ModeManager interface exported from modeManager.ts — they are validation-specific internals, not part of the GeometrySystem contract.

issues.title: cleanup() is optional on GeometrySystem — cleanupInactive must handle missing cleanup
issues.detail: GeometrySystem.cleanup is optional (types.ts:22). The plan's cleanupInactive() description says 'calls cleanup() on all non-active systems' without guarding for the optional. While current systems likely all implement it, the contract doesn't guarantee it.
issues.recommendation: Use optional chaining: `systems[i].cleanup?.()` in cleanupInactive, consistent with how modeManager.ts:77 already does it.

issues.title: Collector should be cleared before renderer.compile() call
issues.detail: If validateShaderCompilation is ever called more than once (e.g., after a WebGL context restore), stale errors from a previous compilation would cause a false failure. The plan doesn't mention clearing the collector before compile.
issues.recommendation: Call errorCollector.clear() at the start of validateShaderCompilation, before renderer.compile().

suggestions.detail: Consider exporting a named interface for the initScene return type (e.g., SceneResult) rather than an anonymous object type. This makes it easier for main.ts and tests to reference without ReturnType<typeof initScene>.

Since the errorCollector is created in initScene but validation happens inside the rAF callback (which fires long after initScene returns), consider whether the collector should also be accessible for context-restore scenarios. The existing onContextRestored handler in scene.ts:64-68 could re-validate after restore.

suggestions.location: Step 4 — test for error recovery path
suggestions.detail: Add a test case that verifies the renderLoop does NOT crash when validateShaderCompilation encounters errors (once the high-severity try/catch fix is applied). This is the most important behavioral contract to test.

stepsAssessment.step2: revise — add errorCollector.clear() before compile, and document that the function should not throw in rAF context
stepsAssessment.step3: revise — resolve interface vs duck-type conflict, add try/catch around validation in renderLoop, guard optional cleanup
stepsAssessment.step4: approved — add one test for error-recovery behavior once step 3 is fixed
